scores.materiality_reasoning: Political participation (proposing resolutions) is a concrete legal right with direct influence on state resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Disqualification from the Assembly affects a broad category of civic and political life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The disability lasts until a debt is paid, which can span decades or be inherited.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Debtor status is a financial condition that can be exited by paying the debt.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Demosthenes is discussing a statute currently in force in 4th-century Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim cites the specific law as an established, verifiable legal fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a debtor does not inherently mean one is barred from speaking; it is a specific legal penalty.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection of the physical person from seizure is a fundamental bodily entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Bodily integrity and freedom from corporal liability are foundational societal axes.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This protection is a stable, lifelong attribute of the status of being a 'freeman'.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Free status is a legal birth-status, very difficult to change (requiring enslavement).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The author contrasts contemporary legal statuses of his own society.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: While used in an argument, it describes well-attested structural differences in Athenian law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being 'free' does not analytically entail protection from all debt-seizure; it is a legal rule.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Tax rates (eisphora) have a direct and significant material effect on wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Taxation is a broad category of legal and economic obligation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Metic tax obligations persist as long as the individual maintains resident alien status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Resident alien (metic) status is a legal birth-status requiring naturalization to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the contemporary tax system of democratic Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim refers to the 'contribution of one-sixth' as a known institutional fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an alien does not inherently define a 1/6th tax rate; it is an external rule.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Regulation of grain transport is a trade restriction with tangible economic consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: The resource is specific to maritime trade and grain logistics.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The restriction applies to all commercial voyages undertaken while a resident/citizen.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Athenians and resident aliens are legal/structural status groups.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Demosthenes quotes a specific, current Athenian trade law.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim quotes the actual text of the law ('It shall be unlawful...').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship/residency does not by definition include grain transport restrictions.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Debt liability directly affects the material value of an inheritance.
scores.generality_reasoning: Inheritance and debt liability are broad categories of property law.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Inheritance and associated liabilities are lifelong transfers of wealth.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Heir status is determined by birth and lineage (being the brother).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The orator argues based on the legal norms governing his own society.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The author asserts this as an 'established legal norm' that the opponent cannot bypass.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an heir does not analytically mean one must pay debts; some systems allow renunciation.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to specialized courts is a concrete legal right that protects material assets.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to mercantile/maritime legal procedures.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Foreigners had this standing whenever they engaged in trade at Athens.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Foreigner/Phaselite status is a fixed ethnicity or birth-based citizenship.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Demosthenes refers to the current 'dikai emporikai' system in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim states equality in mercantile suits as an observed institutional fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a foreigner does not define access to a specific Athenian court.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to reside without a patron (and avoid prosecution) is a concrete legal standing.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a specific procedural requirement (prostasia).
scores.persistence_reasoning: This requirement lasted for the entire duration of a foreigner's residency.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Resident alien (metic) status is a legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the current jurisdiction of the Athenian Polemarch.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the specific charge ('disregard of a patron') under the Polemarch's jurisdiction.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Resident alien status does not inherently mean one must have a 'patron' in all societies.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Physical control and sale of pledged cargo is a tangible property right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific commercial procedure and contract type.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The right is episodic, lasting only for the duration of a specific loan/default.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Lenders are defined by the episodic act of lending in a single transaction.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The text describes laws governing contemporary maritime loans.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim quotes the legal permissions granted to lenders as factual powers.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Granting physical control and sale power is a specific legal choice, not a definition of lending.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Official financial records have evidentiary value with material payoff in court.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to the institutional setting of banking.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Record-keeping is a constant, long-term requirement of the banking profession.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Banker is a durable occupational identity acquired through training and status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The author describes current Athenian banking practices.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes standard professional practice as a generally known fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Record keeping is not what the word 'banker' means, but a rule for the role.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to summon witnesses and sue for damages has direct material consequences in litigation.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the recurring procedure of court trials.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The resource is bounded to the duration of a specific legal case.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The right is tied to the durable legal status of citizenship.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to current Athenian statutes ('as the law provides').
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites specific legal procedures ('depositing a drachma') as simple fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship does not conceptually entail a drachma-deposit rule for witnesses.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Subjection to torture (basanos) is a direct bodily imposition/violation.
scores.generality_reasoning: Torture for evidence was a broad category of legal treatment for slaves.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This liability to physical coercion was a lifelong condition of slave status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a fixed legal status, requiring the rare act of manumission to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the standard legal procedure of democratic Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The orator presents this method of verification as the standard, expected procedure.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A 'slave' is property, but it is a specific rule of law that they must be tortured for testimony.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Liability to impeachment is a concrete legal threat affecting political status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the context of the Assembly and public speaking.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Liability occurs during the episode of making a proposal to the Assembly.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The rule applies to citizens, a stable legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to active Athenian impeachment (eisangelia) laws.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites existing 'laws which declare' the liability for deception.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not inherently mean one faces impeachment for lying.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exclusion from un-coerced testimony is a concrete legal disability with material effects on legal defense.
scores.generality_reasoning: This disability spans all legal proceedings involving slaves.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The inability to testify voluntarily is a lifelong structural feature of slave status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a structural/legal birth-status status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the contemporary binary witness laws in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the binary legal choice (free/deposition vs. slave/torture) as plain fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Exclusion from non-coerced testimony is a legal rule, not the definition of 'slave'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to inherit property is a fundamental material entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Inheritance is a foundational societal axis for wealth transmission.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Inheritance rights are lifelong and structural.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Illegitimacy (nothos) is a fixed status based on the circumstances of birth.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Isaeus cites a contemporary law used in current inheritance cases.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim cites exactly what 'the law ordains' as a factual rule.
scores.tautology_reasoning: In Athens, 'illegitimacy' was defined specifically by the exclusion from kinship and inheritance rights.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to dispose of property (testamentary freedom) is a tangible property right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational property right covering all owned assets.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This legal capacity holds throughout the citizen's adult life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Athenian citizenship is a legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the Solonic laws on wills still in force in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Explicitly states 'This law... permits anyone to dispose of his property'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship does not analytically include the right to dispose of property by will.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Automatic inheritance of the father's estate is a tangible property entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Property transmission through lineage is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This right is a lifelong structural feature of being a legitimate son.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Legitimacy and biological son-ship are immutable birth-fixed statuses.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary Athenian intestate succession laws.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the automatic operation of the law as an unquestioned fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a 'son' does not by definition mean you inherit automatically in all contexts.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to an equal share of the father's property is a tangible wealth entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Part of the foundational axis of inheritance and wealth division.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The share is a permanent transfer of material status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Legitimacy and birth-status are immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites the existing law ('the law ordains') in current force.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Directly cites the law's requirement as the basis for the argument.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Legitimacy does not analytically entail 'equality of shares' with siblings.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to return to an original family affects property claims and religious rights.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the legal and familial status of adopted persons.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This restriction defines much of the life trajectory for an adopted person.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Adopted status is a legal social identity that can be changed only by meeting specific conditions.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the existing Athenian law on adoption and return.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites 'the law does not allow' as a factual legal prohibition.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of adoption does not logically include a bar on returning to a prior family.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Being an heiress (epikleros) determines the transfer of a whole estate.
scores.generality_reasoning: Part of the foundational societal axis of property and gender status.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The status of heiress (or lack thereof) is a permanent life-trajectory event.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Daughter-hood is a biologically and birth-fixed status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary Athenian rules on epikleroi.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the impossibility of the status ('could not be') under law as a fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a daughter does not inherently define when one becomes an 'epikleros'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exclusion from family property and cults has direct material and social consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Exclusion from property and religion are broad, foundational disabilities.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This exclusion was lifelong and structural for bastards.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Bastardy (nothos) is an immutable birth-circumstance.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites a real law established after 403/2 BCE still in force.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the specific timeframe (Archonship of Eucleides) and the legal prescription.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being 'nothos' in Athens meant, by law, having no share in property or cult.
